verify.title: Fix Verification: {status}
verify.subtitle: Comparing against previous analysis
verify.transient_text: One-time spike from an older commit. The metric has since returned to its baseline. Safe to accept.
verify.stabilized_text: An older commit shifted this metric to a new level and it stayed there. The statistical model absorbed the change, but the value differs from the original baseline. Consider whether this shift was intentional.
verify.active_text: This metric is still actively deviating in the latest data. Investigation recommended.

findings.title: Key Findings
findings.empty: No unusual activity detected. Your project's development patterns are within normal ranges.

changes.title: What Changed in Your Codebase
changes.detected: We've detected {n} change{s} that differ from your project's normal patterns. Each change shows what typically happens versus what we observed this time.
changes.accepted_hint: These were previously reviewed and marked as expected. Manage with evo accept --list

card.was_compared: {metric} was {value}, compared to the typical value of {median}.
card.technical_text: The {metric} for this change was {value}. Historically, similar changes had a value of {median}.
card.hint_thisrun: Dismiss now, flag again next analysis
card.after_step1: AI suggests fixes → apply the changes to your code
card.after_step2: Run evo analyze . --verify to re-analyze and compare against this run
card.after_step3: If the change was intentional, click Accept above to dismiss it

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.dedup_note: {a} and {b} are treated as related metrics
patterns.what_are_patterns_text: Patterns are recurring correlations between different areas of your project. When a change in one area (e.g. a deployment) consistently coincides with unusual behavior in another (e.g. code dispersion), we flag it so you can decide if it needs attention.
patterns.what_should_you_do_text: Focus on items marked {action_required} or {needs_attention} first. These indicate patterns that are most likely to affect code quality, stability, or security. Items marked {worth_monitoring} don't need immediate action but should be reviewed if they persist. {healthy_pattern} and {informational} confirm that things are working as expected.

risk_banner.critical_text: One or more patterns indicate serious risks that could affect code quality, stability, or security. Review the items below and address "Action Required" findings before your next release.
risk_banner.concern_text: Some patterns suggest developing issues that could grow into problems if left unaddressed. Review the items marked "Needs Attention" and plan follow-up actions.
risk_banner.watch_text: Several patterns are shifting outside normal ranges. None require immediate action, but monitoring these trends will help you catch issues early.
risk_banner.info_text: Patterns detected are informational or healthy. No issues require attention at this time.
risk_banner.positive_text: All detected patterns are healthy. Your project's development patterns are working well.

investigation.title: Next Steps
investigation.step1_text: Copy the prompt below and paste it into your AI assistant (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT). It will identify root causes and suggest fixes.
investigation.step2_text: Apply the suggested changes. If a deviation was intentional, click Accept on its card above instead.
investigation.step3_text: Run evo analyze . --verify to re-analyze and compare. A verification banner will show which deviations resolved, improved, or persist.
investigation.show_full_hint: Click "Show Full Prompt" to see the complete investigation prompt with evidence...

sources.title: What EE Can See
sources.signals_analyzed: {n} signal{s} analyzed — all within normal range
sources.token_set: Token set. This data is analyzed automatically when running via GitHub Action or GitLab CI.
sources.connected_no_data_default: No events found in the analysis window.
sources.ci_no_runs: No pipeline runs found in the analysis window.
sources.deployment_no_releases: No published releases found in the analysis window.
sources.security_clean: No security alerts found — your dependencies look clean.
sources.error_no_events: No error events found in the analysis window.
sources.testing_no_files: No test result files (JUnit XML) found.

adapters.title: Expand Your Coverage
adapters.intro: Evolution Engine has {n} universal patterns learned from 200+ open-source repositories. The more signal families you connect, the more cross-family patterns can be detected.
adapters.available_subtitle: Enable these adapters to unlock additional signal families and cross-family pattern detection.
adapters.step1: Set the environment variable shown on the adapter card. For example: export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)
adapters.step2: For file-based adapters (Testing, Coverage): generate reports in your project first. For example, pytest --junitxml=junit.xml or pytest --cov --cov-report=xml
adapters.step3: Run analysis: evo analyze . — new adapters are detected automatically
adapters.step4: Verify: evo sources to confirm which adapters are active

footer.ai_disclaimer: This investigation was generated with the assistance of artificial intelligence (Claude by Anthropic). AI-generated content should be reviewed by a developer before acting upon recommendations.
